title: The One With The Pediatrician

jokes.text: Well, I kind of have to, don't I? Because of this stupid thing.
jokes.setup: Monica asks if he has to go to Tulsa for work
jokes.punchline: Chandler dismissively refers to their marriage as 'this stupid thing'
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler self-deprecating humor about his relationship - perfectly in character and economical

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Do you have to go to Tulsa for work?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, I do. But I don't want to leave you.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So don't go.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I have to. It's my job.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, what about us? What about this?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What about this stupid thing?

jokes.text: There's nothing like the support of your loving wife, huh?
jokes.setup: Monica just called their marriage 'this stupid thing'
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically comments on her 'support'
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up sarcasm but fairly predictable response

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, nothing says 'I'm behind you 100 percent' like calling our marriage 'this stupid thing.'

jokes.text: But we were gonna go see the Jets!
jokes.setup: Chandler says he can't make the Jets game due to moving to Tulsa
jokes.punchline: Joey's childish repetition emphasizes his misplaced priorities
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey - cares more about sports than his friend moving away

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: The Jets game? The Jets game?! Chandler, how could you miss the Jets game?

jokes.text: You can't go. I mean, you're the glue that holds this group together. - Really? - Not you.
jokes.setup: Joey says Chandler is the glue holding the group together
jokes.punchline: Monica clarifies she's talking to someone else, not Chandler
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect timing - Chandler's brief moment of validation immediately crushed

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You're the glue holding this group together.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Really? Me?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, I'm talking to someone else.

jokes.text: I call their apartment! - No! No!
jokes.setup: The group learns Monica and Chandler are moving
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately tries to claim their apartment like vultures over a corpse
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey selfishness - perfectly timed and in character

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. So you're moving out? Both of you?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes, we found a place in the suburbs.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Oh my God! When are you leaving?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Next month.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Next month?! I call the apartment!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, you can't just call it.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Why not? I called it! I'm moving in! I get the bedroom, the living room, the kitchen—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, there's a lease. There's an application process.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: An application? Monica, I've been your neighbor for ten years. What do you need, references?

jokes.text: Yeah, just to be sure, I'm gonna call Dr. Wiener.
jokes.setup: Rachel wants to check if she can have caffeine while breastfeeding
jokes.punchline: The pediatrician's unfortunate name 'Wiener'
jokes.explanation: Cheap name humor but sets up running gag throughout episode

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Can I have caffeine while breastfeeding?

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: Of course. I'm Dr. Wiener.

jokes.text: Every time? - Mm-hm.
jokes.setup: Ross realizes Rachel calls the doctor for every little thing
jokes.punchline: Rachel's matter-of-fact confirmation of her neurotic behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's new mother anxiety

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel, you call the doctor for every little thing.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, I like to be informed.

jokes.text: I can see you dialing. I don't understand why... Shh, I'm on the phone! Dr. Wiener?
jokes.setup: Ross tells Rachel she doesn't need to call the doctor
jokes.punchline: Rachel immediately calls while Ross is still talking
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment - Rachel's complete dismissal of Ross while doing exactly what he said not to do

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You don't need to call the doctor.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Hi, yes, I'd like to make an appointment.

jokes.text: A person named 'Wiener.' God, that kills me.
jokes.setup: The doctor's name has been mentioned
jokes.punchline: Chandler's juvenile delight in the name
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Chandler - can't resist juvenile humor even during serious moments

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Dr. Noonan? NOONAN?! That's the best name I've ever heard! I'm gonna request him for everything. Colonoscopy? I want Noonan. Root canal? Noonan. I don't even care what he does, I just wanna say, 'Thanks, Noonan!'

jokes.text: And looks aren't as important as... - Nah, she's gotta be hot.
jokes.setup: Joey claims he's ready for a serious relationship where looks aren't important
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself - she still has to be hot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - tries to be mature but can't help being shallow

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm ready for a serious relationship where looks don't matter.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: She just has to be really, really hot.

jokes.text: Poopack? Ha-ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggests setting Joey up with Sandy Poopack
jokes.punchline: Joey's juvenile reaction to another unfortunate name
jokes.explanation: Another cheap name joke but establishes pattern for the scene

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Sandy Poopak? Hehehehe.

jokes.text: Yeah. All right. Well, that rules out Lana Titwyler.
jokes.setup: Joey laughed at Sandy Poopack's name
jokes.punchline: Phoebe mentions an even more ridiculous name
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the ridiculous names bit

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well I know a guy named Bidet Tron.

jokes.text: No. I'm sorry, are you just used to saying that?
jokes.setup: Monica says 'No' reflexively when Chandler asks if she'll be okay with him gone 4 days
jokes.punchline: Chandler notices her automatic negative response pattern
jokes.explanation: Smart observational humor about Monica's controlling nature

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You'll be okay while I'm gone for four days, right?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, I haven't even finished the question.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh. Sorry. No.

jokes.text: That's fast math. We could use you in Tulsa.
jokes.setup: Monica quickly calculates 208 days out of the year
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry observation about her math skills
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the Tulsa situation with typical Chandler delivery

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: 208 days out of the year.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wow, someone's been doing their math.

jokes.text: You said that without gagging. - I know!
jokes.setup: Monica says she'll go to Tulsa with Chandler
jokes.punchline: They both acknowledge this is growth for her since she usually hates change
jokes.explanation: Nice character growth moment played for comedy

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'll go to Tulsa with you.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah. This is growth for me. I usually hate change.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: Well, excuse me? Oh, yeah? Well, up yours too!
jokes.setup: Rachel is on the phone with someone
jokes.punchline: She's aggressively arguing with what we'll learn is her baby's doctor
jokes.explanation: Sets up the reveal that she's fighting with Dr. Wiener

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, no, no, that is not what the book says! Yes, I have the book. No, I don't care what you went to school for!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, maybe your school was wrong! Have you thought about that?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You know what, I'm gonna get a second opinion. Actually, I'm gonna get a third opinion because I don't trust you!

jokes.text: Who the hell was that? - Dr. Wiener.
jokes.setup: Ross asks who Rachel was just arguing with
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was fighting with her baby's pediatrician
jokes.explanation: Great reveal - the inappropriate phone call was with the baby's doctor

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Who were you just arguing with?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: My baby's pediatrician.

jokes.text: You know what? You sound just like his wife.
jokes.setup: Ross criticizes Rachel for calling the doctor at 3am
jokes.punchline: Rachel reveals the doctor's wife also complained
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how inappropriate Rachel's behavior has become

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel, you called the doctor at 3 in the morning?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, I was worried!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It's 3 AM! That's completely inconsiderate.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh please. His wife called me this morning and said the same thing.

jokes.text: What was it? - Hiccups.
jokes.setup: Ross asks what was wrong with Emma that required a 3am call
jokes.punchline: It was just hiccups - completely normal and non-urgent
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline - reveals how ridiculous Rachel's panic was

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What was wrong with Emma that required a 3am call?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It was just hiccups.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Just hiccups? Do you know what time it is?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, they were really bad hiccups!

jokes.text: Well, not anymore I can't. He fired us!
jokes.setup: Ross says she can't keep calling the doctor for every little thing
jokes.punchline: She's escalated to the point where the doctor fired them as patients
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Rachel's neurosis has reached professional consequences

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You can't keep calling the doctor for every little thing.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? I have a legitimate concern!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel, you called him yesterday because you thought you had a hangnail.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It could have been serious!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, it got serious. The doctor fired us as patients.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: He what?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, he said we're his most high-maintenance patients ever. He actually used the word 'escalated.'

jokes.text: You know, come to think of it, it does feel rubella-like.
jokes.setup: Rachel panics about Ross having rubella around the unvaccinated baby
jokes.punchline: Ross plays into her paranoia with fake medical self-diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Ross feeding into Rachel's medical anxiety for his own amusement

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Actually, I think I might have measles too. And possibly mumps. Oh, and I'm pretty sure my spleen just fell out.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm kidding! I'm just messing with you.

jokes.text: Wiener! Wiener! Wiener! Wiener! Rachel! Great! Now he's gonna know it was me!
jokes.setup: Rachel is yelling 'Wiener' outside the doctor's office
jokes.punchline: Monica yells Rachel's name, making her identifiable to the doctor
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy bit with Monica accidentally making things worse

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wiener! Wiener!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh no.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Ooh. Great. Just the reaction I was hoping for.
jokes.setup: Phoebe shows Joey what she's wearing for their double date
jokes.punchline: Joey's horrified reaction and Phoebe's sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction beat comedy, well executed but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Phoebe, what are you wearing?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What? I think I look great!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You look like you got dressed in the dark at a thrift store.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm sorry, I didn't realize we were going for 'Joey's idea of fashion,' which is basically just whatever makes you look like you're trying too hard.

jokes.text: I can't tell you how I know this, but she is not opposed to threesomes.
jokes.setup: Phoebe is describing Mary Ellen's positive qualities
jokes.punchline: Ends with unexpected sexual information
jokes.explanation: Very Phoebe - casually dropping inappropriate information

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Mary Ellen was my best friend in high school. She was beautiful, smart, kind to animals... and a raging nymphomaniac.

jokes.text: Damn it! Is there no mystery left in romance anymore?!
jokes.setup: Joey asks for Mike's last name after asking his first name
jokes.punchline: Phoebe acts like basic biographical information ruins romance
jokes.explanation: Great Phoebe absurdity - treating a last name as if it's some romantic mystery

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What's your last name?

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Hannigan.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, Mike Hannigan. No! No, that ruins it! It was so much better when he was just Mike. Now he's a guy with a last name. He's a guy with a job, probably a social security number. It's too real, it's too much!

jokes.text: Why'd I have to say Mike? I don't know a Mike! Why couldn't I have said? There's no guys in there!
jokes.setup: Phoebe realizes she promised Joey a date with 'Mike' but doesn't know any Mikes
jokes.punchline: She goes through her purse looking for male names and finds none
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the Mike storyline - Phoebe's impulsive lying catching up with her

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh no, oh no, oh no. I promised Joey a date with Mike and I don't know any Mikes!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, let me look through my purse, maybe I have a Mike in here.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I've got a Brian, a David, a couple of Jons... no Mike.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I've got a harmonica, some gum, a half-eaten sandwich... still no Mike!

jokes.text: How do you know the good ones... from the ones who'll push their penis against your knee?
jokes.setup: Monica is asking how to find a good doctor
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions inappropriate doctor behavior as if it's normal
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly - shocking but fits the show's boundary-pushing

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: How do you find a good doctor?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, you could try asking around.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I tried that. My last doctor told me to lose weight, and then he asked me out.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That's horrible!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know. And he had such good hands too.

jokes.text: I know what she's talking about.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions inappropriate doctor behavior
jokes.punchline: Chandler casually confirms he's experienced this too
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that makes the situation more uncomfortable

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Can you believe my doctor kept hitting on me during my exam?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, mine too. But then I realized he was just checking my prostate.

jokes.text: Her, on the other hand, botched Botox.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions seeing Dr. Gettleman's daughter who said he was fine
jokes.punchline: Implies the daughter's face is visibly messed up from bad cosmetic surgery
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with 'botched Botox' but fairly predictable

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I saw Dr. Gettleman's daughter the other day, and she said he was fine.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, his daughter's face says otherwise.

jokes.text: That I was going to eat him.
jokes.setup: Monica explains Ross had a recurring nightmare as a child
jokes.punchline: The nightmare was about Monica eating him
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - perfectly explains sibling dynamics and Monica's intensity. Economical and memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He had a recurring nightmare about me eating him.

jokes.text: Okay.
jokes.setup: Random stranger at Central Perk responds when Phoebe calls out 'Mike!'
jokes.punchline: His confused but polite response to being randomly addressed
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective setup for the mistaken identity plot

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Mike!

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Oh, uh... hi? Do I know you?

jokes.text: I am Mike. - Attaboy.
jokes.setup: Phoebe coaches the stranger to pretend to be Mike
jokes.punchline: He points out he actually IS Mike, and she treats this as good acting
jokes.explanation: Great Phoebe logic - treating reality as if it's pretending

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, so you're pretending to be Mike. Really commit to it. Make me believe you're Mike.

dialogue.speaker: Stranger
dialogue.line: But I actually am Mike.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's amazing! You're so good at this!

jokes.text: Wow, if I had a nickel for every time somebody's asked me that.
jokes.setup: Mary Ellen asks how Mike and Joey know each other
jokes.punchline: Mike stalls with a cliché phrase instead of answering
jokes.explanation: Weak stalling joke but serves the plot function

dialogue.speaker: Mary Ellen
dialogue.line: How do you two know each other?

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: You know, it's a long story.

jokes.text: From school. - We met in college. I mean high school.
jokes.setup: They're trying to establish their fake friendship history
jokes.punchline: They immediately contradict each other and Mike makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as their lie immediately falls apart

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: We met in college.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: High school.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Right, high school. We were roommates.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: We lived three states apart.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, we visited each other every weekend.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: I've never left New Jersey.

jokes.text: Mike, attorney at law! Ha, ha. Actually, I just gave up my practice. - What? That's the kind of thing you usually run by me. Ha, ha.
jokes.setup: Mike mentions being a lawyer
jokes.punchline: Joey acts like an old friend who should have been consulted about major life decisions
jokes.explanation: Joey overcommitting to the fake friendship creates good cringe

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: I'm a lawyer.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A lawyer? And you didn't think to call me? We go way back!

jokes.text: Slim Pickins. It's a barbecue joint.
jokes.setup: Chandler says Monica found 'Slim Pickins' for restaurant jobs
jokes.punchline: It's literally the name of a restaurant, not a description of few options
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay on the phrase 'slim pickings'

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? It's a real restaurant!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know, but you have to admit, it's a pretty accurate description of the job market right now.

jokes.text: They're looking for somebody to shovel mesquite.
jokes.setup: Monica says 'cook' may be a stretch for this job
jokes.punchline: The actual job is just shoveling wood chips
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how limited the opportunities are

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: 'Cook' may be a stretch for this job. The actual job is just shoveling wood chips.

jokes.text: Well, So Cheesy also has an opening.
jokes.setup: Chandler calls the restaurant name 'cheesy'
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals another restaurant is literally named 'So Cheesy'
jokes.explanation: Good callback that creates a pattern of literal restaurant names

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That name is so cheesy.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Actually, there's a restaurant down the street literally called 'So Cheesy.'

jokes.text: So Cheesy? Whole Hog?
jokes.setup: Pattern of restaurants with literal names established
jokes.punchline: Chandler adds another ridiculous restaurant name to the list
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern but diminishing returns on the bit

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh! What about 'The Place Where We Make Pasta'?

jokes.text: It's gonna be tough to keep kosher in Tulsa.
jokes.setup: All the restaurant options are barbecue/meat-focused
jokes.punchline: Chandler notes the irony for keeping kosher dietary laws
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about cultural differences between NY and Oklahoma

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, this is great. A steakhouse, a barbecue place, and a Brazilian churrascaria. You know what? I'm keeping kosher.

jokes.text: I'll take it!
jokes.setup: Monica is on the phone about a dream job in New York while supposedly moving to Tulsa
jokes.punchline: She immediately accepts without consulting Chandler
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica impulsiveness - classic character-driven comedy

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes! Yes, absolutely! When do I start?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Monica, what about Tulsa?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? Oh, right. Tulsa. Um... I'm not going to Tulsa.

jokes.text: He is alive!
jokes.setup: Rachel sees Dr. Gettleman after Ross claimed he was dead
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic realization that Ross lied
jokes.explanation: Good callback moment but fairly straightforward

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... you're alive?

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Gettleman
dialogue.line: Of course I'm alive.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Ross lied to me!

jokes.text: Would you like a lollipop? - Do you even have to ask?
jokes.setup: Doctor offers lollipop to what appears to be an adult patient
jokes.punchline: Adult enthusiastically accepts pediatric treat
jokes.explanation: Good absurd moment showing adults at pediatrician, sets up Ross reveal

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Gettleman
dialogue.line: Would you like a lollipop?

dialogue.speaker: Adult Patient
dialogue.line: Yes, please!

jokes.text: Mike hit my mom with a car.
jokes.setup: Joey needs to explain why he and Mike had a 'falling out'
jokes.punchline: Invents an extremely serious fake incident
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Joey goes way too far with the fake backstory

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: We had a huge falling out. Mike said something to me that I just... I couldn't get past it.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: He said my haircut made me look like a lesbian.

jokes.text: Please, we're trying to have a conversation.
jokes.setup: Mary Ellen asks if Joey's mom is okay after being hit by a car
jokes.punchline: Joey dismisses this reasonable concern as interrupting conversation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey obliviousness - treating major tragedy as minor social interruption

dialogue.speaker: Mary Ellen
dialogue.line: Is your mom okay? She got hit by a car!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, she's fine. But you're interrupting my story!

jokes.text: We pretend we don't know each other. We played all kinds of games. You remember that one where I punch you in the face for not being cool?
jokes.setup: Joey tries to explain why Mike seemed to not know him
jokes.punchline: Threatens violence while pretending it's a nostalgic game
jokes.explanation: Good cringe as Joey gets increasingly desperate and threatening

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: He didn't recognize me! We went to high school together!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, you know what? I'm gonna find him and I'm gonna play a little game with him. It's called 'Remember when we used to do this?' And then I punch him in the face.

jokes.text: Six. - What are you doing? I said seven!
jokes.setup: Mary Ellen tests their friendship by asking how many sisters Joey has
jokes.punchline: Mike gets it wrong and Joey corrects him mid-lie
jokes.explanation: Good cringe as their deception completely falls apart

dialogue.speaker: Mary Ellen
dialogue.line: How many sisters does Joey have?

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Seven.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I have nine sisters.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Nine! That's what I said.

jokes.text: I didn't hit his mother with a car.
jokes.setup: Mike wants to clarify the false accusation
jokes.punchline: Has to explicitly deny hitting someone's mother with a car
jokes.explanation: Good callback - shows how absurd Joey's lie was

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: I did not hit your mother with a car.

jokes.text: Mary Ellen is really smart and cute and loose. - Hey.
jokes.setup: Phoebe lists Mary Ellen's positive qualities
jokes.punchline: Ends with 'loose' right in front of Mary Ellen, who objects
jokes.explanation: Good cringe - Phoebe's lack of filter creates awkward moment

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You're smart, you're funny, you're kind, you're generous...

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: And you're a little loose.

dialogue.speaker: Mary Ellen
dialogue.line: I am not loose!

jokes.text: I'll stay if you can tell me my name.
jokes.setup: Mary Ellen is leaving the disastrous date
jokes.punchline: Challenges Joey to prove basic knowledge about her
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - reduces Joey's failure to its most basic level

dialogue.speaker: Mary Ellen
dialogue.line: Okay, you know what? I'm leaving.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. I can prove I know things about you.

dialogue.speaker: Mary Ellen
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Name one thing.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You're... a woman.

jokes.text: But it's just a little outside of Tulsa. - Well, how far outside? - Manhattan?
jokes.setup: Monica says the job is 'a little outside' Tulsa
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually in Manhattan - about 1,200 miles away
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - 'a little outside' vs. different time zone

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's a little outside Tulsa.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How far outside?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Manhattan.

jokes.text: I'm gonna miss this hand! - Ha, ha.
jokes.setup: Monica presumably smacks Chandler for his sarcastic comment
jokes.punchline: Chandler treats physical abuse as something he'll be nostalgic about
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Chandler's resignation to Monica's physicality

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm gonna miss this.

jokes.text: it's really just one day. - Um, well, if we can't make it one day, we got real problems, my friend.
jokes.setup: Monica has rationalized four days apart down to one day
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically agrees with her ridiculous math
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler sarcasm calling out Monica's self-serving logic

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So really, when you think about it, one day apart is basically the same as four days apart.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yes, that's exactly how math works.

jokes.text: That's the nicest thing anybody's ever said to me.
jokes.setup: Chandler says she 'chatters like a monkey' when excited
jokes.punchline: Monica takes this animal comparison as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Monica's low standards for compliments from Chandler

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: A monkey? That's so cute! Monkeys are adorable!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, that's not—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm going to start doing that more often. Thank you!

jokes.text: Ross still sees his pediatrician!
jokes.setup: Rachel promised not to reveal Ross's secret
jokes.punchline: Monica immediately spills it to everyone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica - can't keep a secret and loves embarrassing Ross

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, oh my God! You will not believe what I just found out about Ross!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He's in love with Rachel!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: We know.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, Monica, we've known for like, years.

jokes.text: Diagnostician or boo-boo fixer? - Mm.
jokes.setup: Ross defends his pediatrician as a 'brilliant diagnostician'
jokes.punchline: Joey reduces it to childish terms
jokes.explanation: Great Joey line - perfectly captures the absurdity in simple terms

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: He's a brilliant diagnostician!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, he's a pediatrician, Ross. He just looks at kids and goes, 'Yep, that's a kid.'

jokes.text: Does he say that before he sticks his thermometer in your tushy?
jokes.setup: Ross says the doctor makes him feel special as his 'favorite patient'
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out the infantilizing medical procedures
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler snark highlighting the inappropriate nature of adult at pediatrician

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, and I'm sure you're his favorite patient. Right up until he has to give you a rectal exam and realizes you're just like all the other guys.

jokes.text: That was not a security blanket. That was a wall hanging. - It didn't spend much time on the wall!
jokes.setup: Ross defends his college security blanket as 'wall hanging'
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out it was clearly used as a blanket
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to Ross's past immaturity with perfect Chandler delivery

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It's a wall hanging! It's a Navajo weaving!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Really? Because there's still a tag on it that says 'Blanket'.

jokes.text: Really, I'm a pretty nice guy. Just ask my parole officer.
jokes.setup: Mike is trying to convince Mary Ellen he's a good person
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions having a parole officer
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - undermines his 'nice guy' claim immediately

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: I'm a good person, Mary Ellen. I have a parole officer who can vouch for me.

jokes.text: Apparently, I'm not a funny guy.
jokes.setup: Mike's parole officer joke falls flat
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on his failed humor attempt
jokes.explanation: Decent meta-humor but nothing exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: I just told a joke about my parole officer and nobody laughed. Guess I'm better at breaking the law than breaking the ice.

jokes.text: Because I was told that I'd get a free dinner, which I didn't. And that I'd meet a pretty girl, which I did.
jokes.setup: Mike explains why he went along with the fake date scheme
jokes.punchline: Smooth transition from complaint to compliment
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Mike's charm despite the chaos

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Well, I figured if I'm gonna be miserable anyway, I might as well help you guys out. Plus, I gotta say, watching you try to act natural was actually pretty entertaining.

jokes.text: There isn't a piano here. - That wouldn't stand in the way of a true pianist.
jokes.setup: Mike says he can't prove he plays piano without a piano
jokes.punchline: Mary Ellen challenges him to perform anyway
jokes.explanation: Sets up the air piano performance nicely

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: I can't prove I play piano without a piano.

dialogue.speaker: Mary Ellen
dialogue.line: So prove it anyway.

jokes.text: [Mike performs air piano]
jokes.setup: Mike is challenged to play piano without a piano
jokes.punchline: He performs elaborate air piano performance
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that shows Mike's commitment and musical ability

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Without a piano? Fine. *begins elaborate air piano performance with exaggerated hand movements and sound effects* Dun-dun-dun-dun! *dramatic flourish* There. Rachmaninoff.

jokes.text: But right now I'm working on a couple Iron Maiden covers.
jokes.setup: Mary Ellen describes playing 'acoustic, folksy stuff'
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's covering heavy metal with acoustic guitar
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - unexpected musical combination

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I play acoustic, folksy stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, like heavy metal, but acoustic.

jokes.text: Yeah, that's how I know.
jokes.setup: Woman asks if Ross's child is a fan of Waldo books after he helps find Waldo
jokes.punchline: Ross reveals his expertise comes from personal interest, not parenting
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment - Ross inadvertently reveals his childish interests

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: Wow, you're really good at finding Waldo. Does your son love those books?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Actually, I'm not that into them because of him. I've just always been really into them.

jokes.text: Come on, Ross Jr. It's time to go in.
jokes.setup: Ross is trying to maintain the illusion he's there with a child
jokes.punchline: Calls himself 'Ross Jr.' to an imaginary child
jokes.explanation: Great cringe escalation - Ross doubles down on the deception awkwardly

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hey buddy, it's me, Ross Jr.

jokes.text: Hey, I helped you find Waldo!
jokes.setup: Child asks mother what's wrong with Ross
jokes.punchline: Ross desperately defends himself with his earlier helpfulness
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Ross's desperate attempt to seem normal

dialogue.speaker: Child
dialogue.line: Mom, what's wrong with that man?

dialogue.speaker: Mother
dialogue.line: Nothing, honey.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I helped you carry your bags!

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Whoa! Whoa, watch the tongue, people. We got a baby over here.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler are kissing goodbye passionately
jokes.punchline: Joey acts like their PDA will corrupt baby Emma
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey overreaction and inappropriate parental concern

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Hey! There's a baby in the room!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You guys, come on! She's gonna see this!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Do you want her to think that's how people say goodbye?

jokes.text: You know, you're nothing but a big leaver. A big leaver with a stupid suitcase.
jokes.setup: Joey admits he's mad at Chandler for leaving
jokes.punchline: Childish insults including attacking the suitcase
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - childish emotional expression with random object insult

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know what? I am mad at you! You left me here!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, I had to go to London for work.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well, you're... you're stupid! And your hair looks dumb! And that suitcase of yours is ugly and it smells!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Did you just insult my suitcase?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah! It's a stupid suitcase!

jokes.text: Aw, dude, you see right through me!
jokes.setup: Chandler asks if Joey is picking a fight to make leaving easier
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately admits to the transparent emotional strategy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Joey's emotional honesty despite his games

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Are you picking a fight to make leaving easier?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah.

jokes.text: It goes old job... new job... and you.
jokes.setup: Chandler earlier said her priorities were 'old job, me, new job'
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals he's actually third priority after both jobs
jokes.explanation: Great callback that makes Monica's priorities even worse than Chandler thought

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, actually, you're third.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Third? What's third?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Old job, new job, you.

jokes.text: Can I talk now? - Okay, bye. - Joey! - He had to board.
jokes.setup: Joey finally gets a chance to say goodbye to Chandler
jokes.punchline: Monica immediately hangs up, cutting off Joey's moment
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Monica's possessiveness ruins Joey's goodbye

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Chandler! I just wanted to say—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Bye!

tmdb_overview: Rachel's pediatrician drops her as a client because she calls him incessantly to ask about any insignificant issue concerning Emma, so now she must find a new one, which puts Ross in an awkward situation. Meanwhile, Joey arranges a blind date for Phoebe. Also, as Chandler and Monica are preparing to leave for Tulsa, Monica receives a job offer from a prestige restaurant in Manhattan.
insight: Escalation-driven episode relies on character comedy cringe but never quite lands the big laugh.
